title: BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Study Economic Leadership in Mining
summary: The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 is a fully funded opportunity designed to equip women and underrepresented professionals in the mining sector with advanced economic and leadership skills. This program is part of the BRIMM Executive Micro Certificate in Economic Leadership, an intensive educational experience created to shape the next generation of industry leaders. Offered by the Bradshaw Research Institute for Minerals and Mining, this scholarship is funded by external partners who are committed to breaking barriers in education. The goal is to ensure that talented professionals—regardless of their location, gender, or financial situation—can access high-quality mining education and contribute to building a more inclusive and sustainable industry. About the Program The BRIMM Executive Micro Certificate in Economic Leadership is an advanced program that integrates mining expertise with business and economic strategy. It is specifically designed for professionals who want to strengthen their leadership capacity while addressing real-world challenges in the mining sector. Participants will engage in a structured, multi-course learning experience delivered over a single academic term. The program combines theoretical knowledge with practical applications, ensuring that learners can directly implement their insights in their professional environments. Program Structure and Timeline The scholarship covers a single semester during the Fall 2026 term, running from September to December 2026. The program is divided into four progressive courses: BRIMM 101: September 8 – September 24, 2026 BRIMM 102: October 6 – October 22, 2026 BRIMM 103: November 3 – November 19, 2026 BRIMM 104: December 1 – December 17, 2026 Each course builds on the previous one, creating a continuous learning journey. Participants are required to successfully complete each course before progressing to the next. For a detailed breakdown of modules, topics, and curriculum design, explore the full program overview HERE Scholarship Benefits The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship offers a comprehensive package designed to support both learning and career growth: Full Tuition Waiver: Covers all program fees, making the opportunity accessible to high-potential candidates Industry-Relevant Training: Focused on economic leadership within the mining sector Professional Networking: Opportunities to connect with global industry leaders, mentors, and peers Career Advancement: Prepares participants for leadership roles and greater influence within the mining industry What You Will Learn Participants will gain practical and strategic skills that are directly applicable to modern mining challenges, including: Implementing advanced economic strategies to improve efficiency and profitability Integrating sustainable practices to reduce environmental impact Developing data-driven solutions to complex industry problems Enhancing leadership and decision-making capabilities in a global context Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be actively involved in the mining industry, or clearly demonstrate intent to transition into it Show willingness to learn and fully engage with the program Demonstrate motivation to contribute to a better and more inclusive mining industry Have functional English skills for online learning, presentations, and assignments Possess reliable internet access capable of supporting streaming and coursework Who Should Apply This scholarship is ideal for: Women in mining or aspiring to enter the industry Individuals from underrepresented communities Professionals from countries outside the OECD Mid-career professionals seeking leadership and economic expertise Changemakers passionate about sustainability, innovation, and inclusion Selection is based on diversity, equity, and inclusion principles, including gender, country of origin, and the unique perspective each applicant brings to the industry. Terms and Conditions Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the scholarship terms carefully before applying: Duration of Award: The scholarship is valid only for the Fall 2026 semester (September–December 2026). Non-Transferability: Scholarships cannot be transferred to another individual or deferred to another term. Course Completion Requirement: Participants must pass each course to proceed to the next. Failure to meet course requirements will result in immediate termination of the scholarship and ineligibility for future opportunities. For any inquiries regarding the scholarship terms, applicants can contact: Dana Caudle via email at dana.caudle@ubc.ca Application Process The application process is currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply within the official scholarship window. Application Opens: April 28, 2026 Application Deadline: June 15, 2026 Applicants should ensure that all required information is submitted and that their application clearly highlights their experience, leadership potential, and commitment to transforming the mining industry. Click HERE to start application process. For more information about this opportunity, click HERE. Why This Scholarship Matters The mining sector is undergoing a significant transformation, with increasing emphasis on sustainability, innovation, and inclusivity. However, leadership within the industry still lacks sufficient diversity. The BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship addresses this gap by: Empowering underrepresented voices in mining Promoting sustainable and responsible mining practices Encouraging innovative and data-driven decision-making Building a global network of forward-thinking leaders This program is not just an academic opportunity—it is a catalyst for long-term industry change. Discover more scholarship opportunities HERE. The post BRIMM Economics Leadership Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Study Economic Leadership in Mining appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Future Leaders in Women’s Health Research
summary: The NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded PhD opportunity designed to support researchers who are passionate about advancing women’s health. This unique partnership between the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) and Wellbeing of Women aims to develop the next generation of independent research leaders by funding impactful and innovative doctoral studies. This award is part of the NIHR Charity Partnership Programme, which brings together leading research funders and charitable organizations to strengthen collaboration and drive meaningful improvements in healthcare outcomes. About the NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award The Doctoral Award provides funding for individuals to undertake a PhD while conducting high-quality research that contributes to improving health systems, services, and outcomes—particularly in the area of women’s reproductive and gynaecological health. Researchers selected for this award will benefit from being part of a vibrant and supportive research community, gaining exposure to cross-sector collaboration and access to valuable development opportunities. Key Focus Areas Wellbeing of Women is particularly interested in funding research that addresses critical issues in women’s health across the life course, including: Pregnancy, birth, and postnatal care Menstrual and gynaecological health Gynaecological cancers Fertility, contraception, and abortion care Pelvic floor health Menopause Healthy ageing and long-term conditions Sexual health What the Doctoral Award Covers Successful applicants will receive comprehensive funding to support their PhD journey, including: Full Salary Support Covers the researcher’s salary with protected time dedicated to research. PhD Tuition Fees Full coverage of academic fees required for the doctoral programme. Research Costs Funding for all essential research-related expenses. Tailored Training and Development A bespoke programme designed to meet individual career development needs. Why Apply for This Award? This Doctoral Award is more than just funding—it is a career-defining opportunity that enables researchers to: Build a strong foundation as an independent research leader Contribute to impactful, real-world health improvements Collaborate across academic, clinical, and charitable sectors Gain access to mentorship and professional development resources Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be based at an institution located in England Demonstrate a role in or contribution to improving health and wellbeing, healthcare services, or social care Propose research aligned with women’s reproductive or gynaecological health (for this partnership award) Applicants are strongly encouraged to: Review detailed eligibility requirements HERE. Contact Wellbeing of Women to confirm alignment of their research topic if unsure Application Process To apply for the NIHR Charity Partnership Doctoral Award: Follow the standard NIHR application process HERE. Ensure compliance with both NIHR and Wellbeing of Women eligibility requirements Prepare and submit all required documentation before the deadline Click HERE to submit your application. For assistance with your application: Email: personal.awards@nihr.ac.uk To learn more about this opportunity, click HERE. For additional inquiries: Email: research@wellbeingofwomen.org.uk Phone: 020 3697 6346 Important Dates Call Launch: 21 April 2026 Application Deadline: 23 June 2026 at 1:00 PM Award Start Date: Between April and September 2027 The NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026 is an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers dedicated to improving women’s health outcomes. With full financial support, structured development, and access to a collaborative research ecosystem, this programme empowers candidates to make a lasting impact while advancing their academic and professional careers. Discover more PhD opportunities on OFY HERE. The post NIHR–Wellbeing of Women Doctoral Award 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity for Future Leaders in Women’s Health Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund 2026: Up to £5,000 Available for Community Organisations Tackling Inequality in Buckinghamshire
summary: Community organisations working tirelessly to combat disadvantage and inequality in Buckinghamshire now have an opportunity to secure financial support through the Rothschild Foundation’s Grassroots Grants Fund. This funding initiative is designed to empower local organisations that are making a real difference in their communities, particularly in areas such as homelessness, financial hardship, mental health, and disability support. The Fund provides grants of up to £5,000 for one year and is specifically aimed at supporting incorporated grassroots organisations that may be small in scale but are delivering impactful community services. Whether funding is needed for operational activities, projects, or capital expenses, the programme seeks to strengthen organisations addressing urgent social challenges across Buckinghamshire. About the Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund The Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund exists to support local organisations working directly with disadvantaged communities. The programme focuses on grassroots organisations that understand local needs and provide practical solutions to improve lives. The Foundation recognises that many smaller organisations often struggle to secure sustainable funding despite delivering essential community services. Through this grant programme, the Foundation aims to bridge that gap by helping organisations expand or sustain their work. Funding is awarded to organisations that can clearly demonstrate both the need for their services and the impact their projects will have on beneficiaries. Areas of Support The grant programme prioritises organisations working in the following areas: 1. Homelessness Funding is available for projects supporting individuals and families experiencing homelessness or housing insecurity. This may include emergency support services, accommodation assistance, outreach programmes, or rehabilitation initiatives. 2. Financial Hardship Organisations helping people facing economic challenges may apply for funding. Eligible initiatives could include food support programmes, debt advice services, employment assistance, or financial education projects. 3. Physical and Learning Disabilities Community groups supporting individuals with physical disabilities or learning difficulties are encouraged to apply. Funding may support accessibility initiatives, specialised programmes, inclusive community activities, or support services. 4. Mental Health and Wellbeing Mental health services remain a major priority area for the Foundation. Grants may support counselling programmes, wellbeing workshops, peer support initiatives, community mental health projects, or other interventions promoting emotional wellbeing. Funding Available Successful applicants can receive: Grants of up to £5,000 Funding for one year Support for: Project costs Core operational costs Capital costs The Foundation also notes that where funding is requested solely for a capital project, applicants should demonstrate that additional funding sources are contributing toward the total project cost. Reporting Requirements Organisations awarded funding will be expected to submit a report at the end of the grant period. This report allows the Foundation to understand: How the project was implemented The impact achieved Lessons learned Community outcomes generated through the funding This reporting process helps maintain transparency and ensures that grants continue supporting meaningful community change. What the Fund Will Not Support Certain activities and expenses are not eligible for funding under the programme. Applications involving the following will not be considered: Grants benefiting individuals directly Overseas travel Religious or political promotion Animal welfare activities Campaigning work Loan repayments Activities already completed before funding approval Statutory responsibilities of public bodies Applicants should carefully review these exclusions before preparing their applications. Who Can Apply? The programme welcomes applications from a broad range of incorporated non-profit organisations, including: Registered charities Charitable Incorporated Organisations (CIOs) Community Interest Companies (CICs limited by guarantee) Incorporated community groups Societies Sports clubs Importantly, newly registered organisations that do not yet have annual accounts may still apply, making the programme accessible to emerging grassroots initiatives. To qualify, organisations must: Have an annual income below £1 million Be based in Buckinghamshire (excluding Milton Keynes), OR Demonstrate that at least 60% of beneficiaries are based in Buckinghamshire Organisations That Are Not Eligible The following organisations are not eligible to apply: Organisations already holding a Rothschild Foundation grant Uniformed groups Statutory organisations, including Town and Parish Councils Organisations operating primarily in Milton Keynes Organisations working mainly in neighbouring counties For-profit businesses and commercial enterprises Application Process Applications must be submitted through the Rothschild Foundation’s funding portal. Applicants are encouraged to review the guidance provided before starting their application, especially first-time users of the system. Applications can be submitted at any time during the year. However, submissions are reviewed four times annually according to the Foundation’s funding schedule. Application Deadlines and Decision Periods Application Deadline Applications Considered In 22nd May June 27th August October 18th November December 27th January March Applicants will typically be informed of the funding decision within six weeks after each application deadline. Why This Grant Matters Grassroots organisations are often at the forefront of addressing social inequality and supporting vulnerable communities. However, limited funding can restrict their ability to maintain or expand critical services. The Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund provides an important opportunity for local organisations to: Sustain vital community programmes Expand services to reach more beneficiaries Improve operational capacity Strengthen long-term impact Address urgent local social challenges For many small organisations, a grant of up to £5,000 can make a significant difference in delivering meaningful community support. Tips for a Strong Application To improve the chances of success, applicants should: Clearly explain the problem their organisation is addressing. Provide evidence of community need and demand. Demonstrate measurable impact and expected outcomes. Explain how the funding will be used in detail. Show how the organisation is financially sustainable. Ensure eligibility criteria are fully met before submission. Strong applications typically include real examples, data, testimonials, or evidence demonstrating the importance of the proposed work. Contact Information Applicants who need support or clarification regarding the programme may contact the funding team directly: Email: grants@heartofbucks.org Telephone: 01296 330134 Interested organisations are encouraged to begin preparing their applications early to ensure all required information is completed before the next funding deadline. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity click here. Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here. The post Rothschild Foundation Grassroots Grants Fund 2026: Up to £5,000 Available for Community Organisations Tackling Inequality in Buckinghamshire appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AI Student Worker Opportunity at the LEGO Foundation 2026: Build Real-World AI Solutions in a Global Impact Organization
summary: The LEGO Foundation is offering an exciting opportunity for students passionate about artificial intelligence and its real-world application. The AI Student Worker role is designed for individuals eager to use cutting-edge AI tools to improve organizational efficiency while contributing to a mission focused on helping children thrive globally. This role is ideal for students who are not just users of AI tools, but builders—individuals who can design, implement, and scale intelligent solutions that transform how work gets done. About the Role As an AI Student Worker, you will play a central role in driving AI-powered transformation within the Foundation. You will collaborate with teams across the organization to identify inefficiencies and implement practical AI-driven solutions. The position is based in Billund , with flexible working hours ranging between 10–20 hours per week to accommodate your academic schedule. Key Responsibilities Your work will focus on delivering tangible improvements through AI and automation. Responsibilities include: Identifying Process Inefficiencies Analyze workflows across teams Detect repetitive or manual processes suitable for automation Building AI-Powered Solutions Design and develop AI agents, copilots, and automated workflows Utilize tools such as Microsoft Copilot , ChatGPT , and Claude Prototyping and Testing Develop internal tools such as chatbots, reporting systems, and knowledge assistants Test and refine AI solutions based on feedback Scaling Solutions Across Teams Expand successful prototypes into scalable, organization-wide solutions What You Will Gain This role offers hands-on experience in applying AI to solve real organizational challenges. Key benefits include: Practical experience building and deploying AI solutions in a global organization Exposure to real-world automation challenges and workflows Opportunity to collaborate across diverse teams and departments Development of problem-solving and execution skills in AI implementation Contribution to a purpose-driven mission focused on children’s development Eligibility Requirements To qualify for this opportunity, applicants must meet the following criteria: Currently enrolled in a relevant degree program such as AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field Have at least 1.5 years remaining in their studies Required Skills and Experience Hands-on experience with AI tools like Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude, or similar Ability to build automations, workflows, or AI agents using APIs, scripting, or low-code platforms Strong problem-solving skills, especially in structuring ambiguous challenges into actionable AI solutions Willingness to experiment, iterate, and improve solutions continuously Ability to work independently and take ownership of projects Fluency in English and Danish (written and spoken) Work Location and Flexibility Based in Billund, Denmark Shuttle bus service available from Aarhus and Vejle Flexible working hours (10–20 hours per week) Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the position will close once a suitable candidate is selected. Application Deadline: 17 May 2026 Application Requirements Submit your application before the deadline HERE. Include examples of AI solutions, automations, or agents you have built To learn more about this opportunity, click HERE. For inquiries, you can contact the Foundation via email at: people@legofoundation.com About the LEGO Foundation The LEGO Foundation is a Danish corporate foundation dedicated to improving children s lives worldwide. Through partnerships and innovative initiatives, the Foundation works to create environments where children can grow, learn, and thrive. Its mission centers on championing the dignity of childhood and ensuring every child has the opportunity to reach their full potential. Explore more job opportunities on OFY HERE. The post AI Student Worker Opportunity at the LEGO Foundation 2026: Build Real-World AI Solutions in a Global Impact Organization appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Study Water & Sustainability in Europe: Fully & Partially Funded MSc Opportunities for Mexican Students at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education
summary: As global challenges like climate change, water scarcity, and environmental degradation intensify, the demand for skilled professionals in water management and sustainable development continues to grow. For Mexican students eager to contribute to meaningful global solutions, pursuing a Master of Science degree at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education presents a powerful opportunity. Located in the Netherlands, IHE Delft is globally recognized for its excellence in water education, research, and capacity development. The institute offers specialized MSc programmes designed to equip students with both practical and theoretical expertise to tackle real-world water-related challenges. Even more compelling, a wide range of fully and partially funded scholarships are available specifically for Mexican students. About the MSc Programmes at IHE Delft IHE Delft offers two primary postgraduate pathways tailored to different career goals: 1. MSc in Water and Sustainable Development This programme is designed for students who want to apply their knowledge directly to solving pressing global water issues. Key features include: Focus on real-world water challenges and sustainable solutions Courses in water management, governance, and environmental systems Emphasis on practical, impact-driven learning Ideal for professionals seeking careers in policy, consulting, NGOs, and development sectors 2. Research MSc in Water and Sustainable Development This track is best suited for those interested in academic or research careers. Highlights include: Strong focus on scientific research and innovation Preparation for PhD studies or advanced research roles Opportunities to work on cutting-edge water-related research projects Additional Opportunities Joint MSc programmes with international partner universities Exposure to interdisciplinary approaches combining science, policy, and engineering Why Choose IHE Delft? Studying at IHE Delft offers far more than academic advancement—it provides a transformative global experience. Key Benefits: World-Class Expertise: Learn from leading experts in water science, engineering, and policy Global Learning Environment: Study alongside students from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds Real-World Focus: Address urgent global issues such as climate resilience, sanitation, and water governance Strong Professional Network: Build connections with international organizations, governments, and industry leaders Career Advancement: Gain skills that are highly valued across global development sectors Scholarships Available for Mexican Students A variety of funding opportunities are available to support Mexican students in pursuing their studies. These scholarships may cover tuition fees, living expenses, or both. Scholarships Specifically for Mexican Students SECIHTI: Posgrado en Ciencia y Humanidades en el Extranjero 2026 Deadline: 22 May 2026 Click here for more and to apply. Fundación Esposos Rodríguez Deadline: 29 May 2026 Click here for more and to apply. Brockmann IHE Delft Scholarships Deadline: 25 September 2026 Note: Applicable for MSc programmes starting in 2027/2028 Click here for more and to apply. A Gateway to Global Impact Graduates from IHE Delft are equipped to become leaders in addressing some of the world’s most critical water and sustainability challenges. Whether working in government agencies, international NGOs, research institutions, or private sector organizations, alumni play a vital role in shaping a more sustainable future. This opportunity is especially valuable for Mexican students who want to gain international exposure while contributing solutions that can be applied both globally and locally. For more global scholarship opportunities Click here The post Study Water Sustainability in Europe: Fully Partially Funded MSc Opportunities for Mexican Students at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Non-V4 Experts at IIR Prague
summary: The Institute of International Relations Prague (IIR), in cooperation with the Think Visegrad platform, is pleased to announce the opening of applications for the Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026 . This prestigious fellowship offers researchers, analysts, and policy experts from non-Visegrad countries a unique opportunity to engage with leading Central European think tanks, conduct high-impact policy research, and contribute to strategic regional dialogue. Think Visegrad is not merely a fellowship program — it is a structured think tank platform designed to foster meaningful dialogue on issues of strategic regional importance. Established in 2012 by eight think tanks from the Visegrad Group countries (Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, and Slovakia), the platform has consistently provided actionable recommendations to V4 governments, the annual presidencies of the Group, and the International Visegrad Fund. The platform also functions as a bridge between V4 and non-V4 experts, offering visiting fellowships to researchers from outside the region. For 2026, eight visiting fellowships will be awarded across the core network of think tanks that constitute the Think Visegrad platform. Each selected fellow will be hosted by one of these institutions, gaining direct access to the host institute’s research environment, expert networks, and ongoing activities. Fellowship Duration and Format The duration of each fellowship is flexible, ranging from 6 to 8 weeks . However, all fellows are required to spend a minimum of 4 weeks in-person at the hosting institution. The exact schedule and balance between in-person and remote work is determined through agreement with the host institute. This hybrid structure allows fellows to immerse themselves in the local policy environment while maintaining flexibility for their ongoing professional commitments. Financial Support and Coverage Selected fellows receive comprehensive financial support to ensure a productive and stress-free research stay. The breakdown of funding is as follows: Lump sum of €3,400 – This amount is provided to cover all necessary costs and expenditures related to the fellow’s stay at the hosting institute, including: Lodging / accommodation Local transportation Daily living expenses Health and social insurance (fellow’s responsibility) Any applicable taxes and related fees Additional travel reimbursement of up to €500 – Think Visegrad covers the cost of international travel from the fellow’s home country to the hosting institute. This reimbursement is provided separately and can be claimed up to the maximum amount of €500. Fellows should note that they are personally responsible for paying all taxes, fees, and insurance premiums (including health and social insurance) from the €3,400 lump sum. It is strongly recommended that applicants budget accordingly and consult local regulations regarding short-term research stays. Research Topics and Focus Areas Fellowships are available across a broad spectrum of research areas within: Political science International relations Economics However, selected topics must complement the existing research focus of the hosting institute. Therefore, applicants are highly recommended to visit the website of their preferred host institute before submitting an application. Understanding the institute’s ongoing projects, publications, and strategic priorities will significantly strengthen the relevance and competitiveness of your proposal. Each fellow is expected to achieve two primary deliverables during their fellowship period: A policy paper – A substantive, evidence-based document addressing a pre-agreed topic relevant to the Visegrad region and the host institute’s expertise. One public presentation – A seminar, lecture, or workshop delivered at the hosting institute, allowing the fellow to share their findings with local researchers, policymakers, and the broader expert community. Eligibility Criteria Before applying, please ensure you meet the following requirements: You must be an expert fellow from a non-Visegrad country (i.e., not a citizen or permanent resident of Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland, or Slovakia). Priority is given to applicants with a proven track record of significant contribution in their field of research. High-quality project proposals from junior and mid-career researchers are also duly considered. Previous Think Visegrad Fellows are not eligible to apply again. Applications from past fellows will be automatically dismissed without review. There is no strict age limit or minimum degree requirement, although a strong academic or professional background in political science, international relations, economics, or a closely related discipline is expected. Host Institutions (Core Network Think Tanks) The eight visiting fellowships are distributed across the core network think tanks that coordinate the Think Visegrad platform. While the specific list of host institutions for 2026 should be confirmed on the official website, the core network traditionally includes leading V4 research centers with coordinating abilities. The platform remains open to cooperation with other think tanks from all V4 countries, but the 2026 fellowships will be hosted exclusively by members of this core network. Applicants are strongly encouraged to research each host institute’s thematic focus, ongoing projects, and available mentorship before indicating preferences in their application. Application Requirements All application dossiers must be submitted as three separate PDF files . Incomplete applications or files submitted in other formats may not be considered. The required documents are: Application form – The official Think Visegrad Fellowship application form (available for download from the program’s website). Structured CV – A detailed curriculum vitae that includes a complete list of publications, previous research positions, academic degrees, and relevant professional experience. Use clear headings and chronological organization. Motivation letter – Maximum 300 words. This letter should explain: Why you are interested in the Think Visegrad Fellowship How your research aligns with the host institute’s focus What policy paper topic you propose to develop during the fellowship What you hope to gain from and contribute to the Visegrad research community All three PDF files must be sent by email to the following address: thinktank@sfpa.sk Application Deadline The deadline for submission is May 15, 2026 . Applications received after this date will not be considered. Late or incomplete submissions will be automatically disqualified. Selection Timeline and Process While the official selection timeline is not published in the call, applicants should expect the following general process: Initial eligibility screening Review of project proposals and CVs by the core network think tanks Potential interviews (virtual or in-person) with shortlisted candidates Final selection and matching with host institutes Notification of results (likely by mid-to-late 2026, given the Summer/Autumn/Winter fellowship periods) Fellowships are available for three seasonal periods in 2026: Summer 2026 Autumn 2026 Winter 2026 Selected fellows will work with their host institute to determine exact start and end dates within the agreed seasonal window. Why Apply for the Think Visegrad Fellowship? This fellowship offers more than financial support. It provides: Access to one of Central Europe’s most influential think tank networks An opportunity to publish a policy paper with regional visibility A platform to present your research to V4 policymakers and experts Networking with eight core V4 think tanks and the International Visegrad Fund A chance to understand the political, economic, and security dynamics of the Visegrad region from the inside For researchers looking to expand their policy impact, build cross-border collaborations, and gain firsthand experience in a strategically important European region, the Think Visegrad Fellowship is an exceptional opportunity. Important Notes for Applicants Do not submit your application to individual host institutes. All applications must go through the central email: thinktank@sfpa.sk Ensure each of the three PDF files is clearly named (e.g., “LastName_ApplicationForm.pdf”, “LastName_CV.pdf”, “LastName_MotivationLetter.pdf”) The €3,400 lump sum is paid as one amount. Fellows are responsible for all local arrangements including accommodation, insurance, and tax compliance. International travel reimbursement (up to €500) is processed separately. Keep all receipts and travel documentation. You must be available to spend a minimum of 4 continuous weeks in-person at the hosting institute. The fellowship does not cover family members or dependents. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Think Visegrad Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Non-V4 Experts at IIR Prague appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026: Funding for High-Impact Journalism Worldwide
summary: The Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026 offer a unique and flexible funding opportunity for journalists seeking to produce in-depth, high-impact stories on underreported global issues. Designed to support ambitious reporting projects across all regions and topics, this programme empowers storytellers to bring critical issues to light through innovative and engaging journalism. Open to both freelance and staff journalists worldwide, these grants provide financial support, editorial backing, and a platform to amplify stories that matter. About the Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants The programme is administered by the Pulitzer Center , a globally recognized institution dedicated to supporting quality journalism that addresses systemic challenges and underreported topics. Unlike many traditional funding opportunities, the Global Reporting Grants are highly flexible. There are no thematic or geographic restrictions , allowing applicants to explore a wide range of issues—from climate change and conflict to governance, health, and social justice. The initiative encourages bold storytelling that not only informs but also engages audiences through creative and multimedia approaches. Funding and Financial Support The Pulitzer Center provides financial support tailored to each project’s needs. Most grants range between USD 5,000 and USD 10,000 Funding is based on a detailed and realistic project budget Payments are typically disbursed in two stages: Initial funding upon approval Final payment upon submission of completed work This structure ensures that journalists have the resources needed to conduct thorough reporting while maintaining accountability for project delivery. Programme Focus and Supported Formats The Global Reporting Grants support a wide range of journalistic formats, reflecting the evolving nature of storytelling in the digital age. Supported formats include: Print and digital journalism Photojournalism Audio and radio storytelling Video reporting and short documentaries Projects that integrate multimedia elements, data journalism, and innovative storytelling techniques are particularly encouraged. The goal is to produce compelling content that reaches diverse audiences and drives meaningful conversations. Eligibility Criteria The programme is open to a broad and diverse group of applicants: Staff journalists and freelance journalists worldwide Writers, photographers, audio producers, and filmmakers Individual applicants or collaborative teams Applicants must demonstrate: A clear and feasible reporting plan Strong editorial partnerships or publication strategy The ability to deliver impactful, high-quality journalism Importantly, there are no restrictions on nationality, topic, or geographic focus , making this one of the most accessible global journalism grants available. Application Process Applicants are required to submit their proposals through the official Pulitzer Center platform. A strong application should include: A detailed project proposal A realistic and itemized budget Evidence of editorial partnerships or publishing plans Samples of previous work (if required) Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official guidelines before submitting their proposals to ensure compliance with all requirements. Deadline Deadline: Rolling (applications accepted year-round) This rolling deadline allows journalists to apply at any time, making it easier to align submissions with project readiness and editorial timelines. Why Apply for the Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants? This programme stands out for its flexibility, accessibility, and global reach. It not only provides financial support but also connects journalists with a network of editors, educators, and media partners. For journalists passionate about uncovering underreported stories and making a real-world impact, the Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants offer a powerful platform to turn ideas into influential journalism. Start Your Application If you have a compelling story idea that deserves global attention, this is your opportunity to secure funding and bring it to life. Prepare your proposal, build your editorial partnerships, and submit your application through the official Pulitzer Center platform. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Pulitzer Center Global Reporting Grants 2026: Funding for High-Impact Journalism Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Renaissance Graduate Programme 2026 in Nigeria: Launch Your Career in Africa’s Energy Future
summary: The Renaissance Graduate Programme 2026 is a premier career opportunity designed for ambitious graduates seeking to build a meaningful and impactful career in Africa’s fast-evolving energy sector. Offered by Renaissance Africa Energy Company Limited , this programme goes beyond a traditional entry-level job—it is a structured pathway to developing future leaders who will contribute to Africa’s industrialisation and energy security. Based in Port Harcourt , this full-time, permanent programme combines hands-on experience, professional development, and exposure to real-world energy projects that shape the continent’s future. About the Renaissance Graduate Programme At its core, the Renaissance Graduate Programme is built on the belief that people are the driving force behind transformation. The company fosters an inclusive, high-performance, and collaborative work culture where young professionals are empowered to innovate, grow, and lead. As a graduate trainee, you will join a dynamic community of forward-thinking professionals working on impactful energy solutions. The programme is designed to equip you with critical technical and leadership skills while providing the opportunity to contribute to sustainable energy development across Africa. Why Choose Renaissance? Joining Renaissance means becoming part of a company that prioritizes innovation, sustainability, and human capital development. A Culture of Growth and Excellence The organization promotes a strong “learner mindset,” encouraging continuous development and curiosity. Guided by its CRISP values—Collaboration, Respect, Integrity, Safety, and Performance—you will thrive in an environment that supports both personal and professional growth. Real Impact from Day One Unlike many graduate roles, this programme gives you immediate exposure to real-world projects. You will actively contribute ideas, solve problems, and work on initiatives that address Africa’s most pressing energy challenges. Mentorship and Industry Exposure You will learn directly from experienced professionals and industry leaders, gaining insights that accelerate your career trajectory and position you as a future leader in the energy sector. Key Benefits of the Programme The Renaissance Graduate Programme offers a comprehensive set of benefits designed to fast-track your career: Accelerated Development Through the Renaissance Graduate Academy, you will participate in structured, high-impact training programmes that enhance both technical expertise and leadership capabilities. Diverse Exposure You will collaborate with professionals from different disciplines, cultures, and backgrounds, broadening your perspective and strengthening your adaptability. Holistic Support The programme invests in your long-term success by supporting your career development, continuous learning, and personal wellbeing. Industry-Led Learning Gain practical knowledge and business acumen by working closely with experts shaping Africa’s energy landscape. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Renaissance Graduate Programme, applicants must meet the following requirements: Academic Qualifications Applicants must hold a bachelor’s degree with a minimum of Second Class Upper Division (2:1) or higher in one of the following fields: Engineering: Electrical, Chemical, Mechanical, Civil/Structural, Petroleum, Material/Metallurgical Computing IT: Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, Cybersecurity, Artificial Intelligence, Information Systems Geosciences: Geology, Geophysics, Earth Sciences, Geoinformatics, Geomechanics Natural Applied Sciences: Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Chemistry, Environmental Science Additional Requirements Strong communication skills (written and verbal) Excellent analytical and teamwork abilities Completion of the National Youth Service Corps programme Valid NYSC discharge or exemption certificate issued no earlier than 2023 Required Documents Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents in PDF format only : One-page Curriculum Vitae (CV) Bachelor’s degree certificate (and Master’s if applicable) NYSC completion or exemption certificate Government-issued birth certificate or passport data page Failure to submit the correct documents will result in disqualification. Application Process All applications must be submitted online through the official Renaissance job portal. Submit only one application (multiple submissions lead to automatic disqualification) Carefully review all information before submission Ensure accuracy and honesty—any false or misleading information will result in disqualification at any stage Due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted during each stage of the recruitment process. Important Deadline Application Closing Date: 13th May 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and ensure all required documents are correctly uploaded. Start Your Career in Energy Today The Renaissance Graduate Programme is more than just a job—it is an opportunity to shape the future of Africa’s energy sector while building a rewarding and purpose-driven career. If you are a high-achieving graduate ready to make an impact, this programme offers the perfect platform to grow, lead, and contribute to Africa’s development. title: Afreximbank-Edordu Research Competition 2026: Empowering Africa’s Next Generation of Policy Thinkers
summary: The Afreximbank-Edordu Research Competition 2026 is a prestigious academic initiative designed to spotlight and support the brightest postgraduate minds across Africa and eligible Caribbean states. Focused on shaping the continent’s economic future, this competition invites rigorous, original research on critical themes such as trade, finance, regional integration, and sustainable development. Far from being a purely academic exercise, this competition offers participants a rare opportunity to transform their research into real-world policy impact—placing young scholars at the heart of Africa’s economic transformation. About the Competition Organized by the African Export-Import Bank , the competition honors the legacy of Christopher Edordu , the Bank’s pioneer president. His vision emphasized African economic sovereignty, institutional strength, and intellectual leadership—values that continue to guide Afreximbank today. The competition goes beyond traditional academic recognition. The top three research papers are transformed into actionable policy briefs, presented directly to Afreximbank leadership, and showcased at major institutional events. This ensures that winning ideas actively contribute to shaping policy and practice. Why You Should Participate Participating in the Afreximbank-Edordu Research Competition offers more than recognition—it is a launchpad for influence and career growth. Continental Recognition Participants gain visibility among Africa’s leading policymakers, academics, and development experts, positioning themselves as emerging thought leaders. Substantial Financial Support The competition offers a USD 20,000 prize , along with additional support for books and tuition—helping winners advance their academic and professional journeys. Policy Impact Winning research is translated into policy briefs, giving participants the opportunity to directly influence economic strategies and decisions. A Public Platform Finalists present their work at high-level Afreximbank events, amplifying their ideas across influential networks in Africa and the diaspora. Eligibility Criteria The competition is open to a specific group of high-level academic participants: Postgraduate students (Master’s or PhD) Currently enrolled in an accredited tertiary institution Studying in Africa or an eligible CARICOM state Holding nationality from an African country or an eligible CARICOM member state Eligible CARICOM countries include Antigua and Barbuda, The Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Haiti, Jamaica, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, and Suriname. Submission Requirements Applicants must adhere to strict academic and formatting standards: Submit original, unpublished research not under review elsewhere Address one of the competition’s officially announced themes Write in any African Union official language (Arabic, English, French, Portuguese, Spanish, or Kiswahili) Ensure papers are between 3,000 and 5,000 words (excluding references, tables, and figures) Use a recognized citation style such as APA, Chicago, or Harvard Submit the paper in PDF or Word format via the official competition portal All submissions will be translated into English for evaluation by the jury. Competition Structure The competition unfolds in two rigorous and highly competitive stages: Stage 1: Submission and Jury Review Participants submit their research papers through the official portal. Each submission undergoes: Eligibility and formatting checks Plagiarism screening Anonymous evaluation by a jury Papers are assessed based on originality, methodological rigor, clarity, relevance to development, and alignment with Afreximbank’s mission. Stage 2: Policy Brief and Presentation The top three candidates advance to the final stage, where they: Develop an actionable policy brief from their research Present their findings at an Afreximbank-designated forum Receive formal approval from the Bank’s Internal Working Group before announcement This stage transforms academic work into practical, implementable solutions. Competition Timeline The competition follows a structured lifecycle for 2026, with key milestones including submission deadlines, evaluation phases, and final presentations. Deadline: Not explicitly specified (applicants should monitor the official competition portal for exact dates and updates). Prizes and Recognition The Afreximbank-Edordu Research Competition offers recognition at multiple levels, with the top three winners receiving: A share of the USD 20,000 prize Financial support for academic resources and tuition Opportunities to present at prestigious Afreximbank events Direct engagement with policymakers and institutional leaders Why This Competition Matters Africa’s economic future depends on innovative ideas, rigorous research, and bold policy thinking. This competition bridges the gap between academia and policymaking by empowering young scholars to contribute meaningfully to real-world challenges. For postgraduate students passionate about development, trade, and economic transformation, the Afreximbank-Edordu Research Competition represents a unique opportunity to gain recognition, influence policy, and shape the continent’s trajectory. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Afreximbank-Edordu Research Competition 2026: Empowering Africa’s Next Generation of Policy Thinkers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Albuquerque Community Foundation Scholarships 2026–2027: A Complete Guide to Funding Your Education in New Mexico
summary: Pursuing higher education can be financially challenging, but scholarship opportunities like those offered by the Albuquerque Community Foundation (ACF) are designed to ease that burden and open doors for students across New Mexico. For the 2026–2027 academic year, ACF has officially opened its scholarship and student aid programs, providing a wide range of funding opportunities tailored to diverse academic paths, backgrounds, and aspirations. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about these scholarships—from eligibility and application steps to key benefits and important deadlines. About the Albuquerque Community Foundation Scholarships The Albuquerque Community Foundation is a well-established philanthropic organization committed to supporting education and empowering future generations. Through its scholarship programs, the foundation partners with donors—individuals, families, and organizations—who are passionate about helping students achieve their academic goals. Each scholarship fund reflects the donor’s unique values, interests, and priorities. This means that the available scholarships cover a broad spectrum of fields, backgrounds, and eligibility criteria, ensuring that many students can find opportunities aligned with their personal and academic journeys. Who Can Apply? The scholarship programs are open to: High school students preparing to enter college Current college students seeking financial support to continue their education Applicants must complete an eligibility questionnaire before accessing the full application. This initial step helps match students with the most relevant scholarship opportunities based on their profile. How the Application Process Works The application process is structured to be efficient and user-friendly while ensuring that applicants are matched with suitable scholarships. Step 1: Create a Student Account Applicants must first create an account through the scholarship portal. This account will serve as the hub for completing applications, tracking progress, and receiving updates. Step 2: Complete the Eligibility Questionnaire Before accessing specific scholarship applications, students are required to fill out an eligibility questionnaire. This step is essential, as it determines which scholarships the student qualifies for. Step 3: Submit Applications Once matched with eligible programs, students can proceed to complete and submit their applications. Requirements may vary depending on the scholarship, including essays, transcripts, recommendation letters, or financial information. Scholarship Diversity and Flexibility One of the standout features of the Albuquerque Community Foundation scholarships is their diversity. Because each program is donor-funded, eligibility requirements and focus areas vary widely. Some scholarships may prioritize: Academic excellence Financial need Community involvement Specific career fields Demographic or geographic criteria This diversity increases the chances for students from different backgrounds to find meaningful financial support. Privacy and Data Protection Applicants can apply with confidence knowing that all personal and financial information submitted is handled with strict confidentiality. The Albuquerque Community Foundation maintains all nonpublic information securely and ensures it is used solely for scholarship evaluation purposes. Additional Opportunities: Establishing a Scholarship Fund Beyond applying for scholarships, the foundation also provides opportunities for donors to create their own scholarship funds. Individuals, families, and organizations can collaborate with ACF to design programs that reflect their philanthropic goals and support deserving students. Important Deadlines Unlike many scholarship programs with a single deadline, the Albuquerque Community Foundation scholarships operate on multiple timelines depending on the specific program. Deadline: Varies by scholarship program (applicants should consult the official deadline list for each opportunity). It is highly recommended that applicants begin the process early to ensure they meet all requirements and deadlines. Why You Should Apply Applying for ACF scholarships is more than just seeking financial aid—it’s about joining a community that believes in your potential. With a wide range of scholarships available, personalized eligibility matching, and strong donor support, this program is an excellent opportunity for students in New Mexico to advance their education without financial constraints. Start Your Application Today If you are a student in New Mexico looking to fund your education, now is the time to take action. Begin by creating your student account, complete the eligibility questionnaire, and explore the scholarship opportunities available to you. Education is an investment in your future—and with the support of the Albuquerque Community Foundation, that future is within reach.
